column1: Bobby Witt Jr. stands out as the top pick, riding a strong recent stretch and recording a hit in nearly 80% of his games, with a favorable platoon matchup and a slight park boost. Yandy Diaz offers similar reliability from the leadoff spot, consistently finding hits and facing a hittable lefty. Brandon Marsh also draws a soft matchup at home, but his recent production lags behind the leaders. Witt’s combination of consistency and matchup makes him the safest choice for Beat The Streak.
